mission.title: Install and Configure Elastic Cloud on Kubernetes (ECK)
mission.description: Elastic Cloud on Kubernetes (ECK) automates the deployment and management of Elastic Stack components on Kubernetes. It is ideal for users who want to run Elasticsearch, Kibana, and other Elastic services in a Kubernetes environment with ease. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential resource overhead.

steps.title: Add the ECK Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Elastic Helm repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install ECK.

```bash

helm repo add elastic https://helm.elastic.co

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Elastic Helm repository.
> Updates the Helm repository to fetch the latest charts.

steps.title: Install Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for ECK to manage Elastic Stack resources. This step installs them.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://download.elastic.co/downloads/eck/2.14.0/crds.yaml

```

> This command installs the required CRDs for ECK.

steps.title: Install the ECK operator
steps.description:
This step installs the ECK operator in the 'elastic-system' namespace, which manages the Elastic Stack resources.

```bash

helm install eck-operator elastic/eck-operator --namespace elastic-system --create-namespace --version 2.14.0

```

> Installs the ECK operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create an ElasticSearch cluster
steps.description:
This step creates an Elasticsearch cluster using a Custom Resource (CR) definition.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: default
spec:
  version: 8.6.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.allow_mmap: false
EOF

```

> Creates a 3-node Elasticsearch cluster.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: default
spec:
  version: 8.6.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.allow_mmap: false
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Elasticsearch cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Elasticsearch cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get elasticsearch -n default

```

> Check the status of the Elasticsearch cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the ECK operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the ECK operator and all associated resources. Data loss may occur if Elasticsearch clusters are not deleted first.

```bash

kubectl delete elasticsearch my-elasticsearch -n default

helm uninstall eck-operator -n elastic-system

```

> Delete the Elasticsearch cluster to prevent data loss.
> Uninstalls the ECK operator.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the ECK operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your data and ensure your clusters are healthy. This step upgrades the ECK operator.

```bash

kubectl get elasticsearch -n default -o json > backup-elasticsearch.json

helm upgrade eck-operator elastic/eck-operator --namespace elastic-system --version 2.14.0

kubectl get elasticsearch -n default

```

> Backup the current Elasticsearch configuration.
> Upgrades the ECK operator to the specified version.
> Verify that the Elasticsearch cluster is still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get elasticsearch -n default -o json > backup-elasticsearch.json

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Elasticsearch cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Elasticsearch cluster may not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource requests/limits in the Elasticsearch CR.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Elasticsearch'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Elasticsearch service may not be reachable due to network policies.

**Fix:**
Check and update the network policies to allow traffic to the Elasticsearch service.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD validation failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRD version may not match the installed ECK operator version.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the CRDs are updated to match the ECK operator version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient nodes available'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may not be enough nodes in the cluster to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up the Kubernetes cluster to add more nodes.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Elasticsearch 9.x, improved TLS certificate management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your Elasticsearch CR to use the new version and apply the changes.

resolution.summary: You should see the Elasticsearch cluster status as 'Health: green' when running `kubectl get elasticsearch -n default`.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: default
spec:
  version: 8.6.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.allow_mmap: false
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher with sufficient resources.
